name: La Fuente de los 100 Caños – circular desde Salinas por Villanueva del Trabuco y las Lagunas de Archidona
region_intro: Mountain biking around Archidona, Spain, offers diverse terrain at the foothills of the Sierra de Gracia mountain range. The region features rolling olive groves, picturesque meadows, and mountainous areas. Notable natural features include the unique Lagunas de Archidona Nature Reserve with its two lakes and the Hoz del Arroyo Marín river canyon. Geological points of interest like El Brosque, known for its sinkholes, also characterize the landscape. Best mountain bike trails around Archidona The most popular mountain bike trail is La Fuente de los 100 Caños – circular desde Salinas por Villanueva del Trabuco y las Lagunas de Archidona, a 24.3 miles (39.2 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete, passing by the Lagunas de Archidona. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Hotel La Bobadilla loop from Estación de Salinas, a moderate 28.9 miles (46.6 km) path. This route traverses varied landscapes, including olive groves and open countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Plaza La Victoria – Archidona loop from Archidona, a 18.1 miles (29.1 km) trail leading through the foothills of Sierra de Gracia, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Mountain biking around Archidona is defined by the Sierra de Gracia, rolling olive groves, and the Lagunas de Archidona. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Archidona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 240 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Archidona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Archidona?
A: Archidona offers a robust network of over 15 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. The komoot community has explored 18 tours in total, with options ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Archidona?
A: The terrain around Archidona is quite varied, situated at the foothills of the Sierra de Gracia mountain range. You'll encounter rolling olive groves, picturesque meadows, and more mountainous areas. The landscape also features unique geological formations like sinkholes in areas such as El Brosque, and river canyons like the Hoz del Arroyo Marín.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Archidona?
A: Yes, there are options for beginners. While most trails are moderate, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. These routes typically feature less challenging gradients and smoother paths, making them ideal for those new to mountain biking or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Archidona?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Archidona offers at least one difficult mountain bike trail. These routes typically feature steeper climbs, technical descents, and more rugged terrain, providing a demanding experience amidst the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails pass by significant natural features and landmarks. You might encounter the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/388446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred‑Spout Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, the geologically significant Lagunas de Archidona Nature Reserve, or the scenic Hoz del Arroyo Marín river canyon. Some routes also traverse areas with old mines and the Guadalhorce River section.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Archidona?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Archidona are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994294753/la-fuente-de-los-100-canos-circular-desde-salinas-por-villanueva-del-trabuco-y-las-lagunas-de-archidona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuente de los 100 Caños – circular desde Salinas por Villanueva del Trabuco y las Lagunas de Archidona&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that takes you past the Lagunas de Archidona.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Archidona's trails?
A: The mountain bike trails in Archidona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the varied landscapes, which include olive groves, meadows, and mountainous sections, as well as the well-maintained network of trails suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Is Archidona suitable for family-friendly mountain biking?
A: Archidona offers a range of trails, including easier options that could be suitable for families. While specific family-friendly routes are not explicitly highlighted, the presence of easy and moderate trails suggests that families with some biking experience can find enjoyable paths, especially those that explore the less challenging olive groves and meadows.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Archidona?
A: Archidona, located in Málaga, Spain, generally enjoys a mild climate. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are often considered the best times for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and less intense heat than the summer months. Winter can also be suitable, though some higher elevation areas might be cooler.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or cultural points of interest?
A: Yes, some routes offer a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. For instance, the historic Stage 6: Villanueva de Algaidas - Archidona follows an old Roman road, featuring Roman Milestones and passing through the Arcas Mountains. Additionally, the town of Archidona itself boasts historical sites like the Castle and Walled Enclosure on Sierra de Gracia, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that include views of the Sierra de Gracia?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Archidona are defined by their proximity to the Sierra de Gracia. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10298537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza La Victoria – Archidona loop from Archidona&lt;/a&gt; leads directly through the foothills of Sierra de Gracia, offering excellent views of the mountain range and the surrounding countryside.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or places to eat along the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific cafes directly on the trails are not detailed, Archidona and its surrounding villages offer various amenities. Many routes start or pass near towns where you can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. It's advisable to plan your route and check for facilities in the towns you'll pass through.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Archidona?
A: Among the listed routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31550509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archidona – Plaza Ochavada of Archidona loop from Archidona&lt;/a&gt; is a notable long-distance option, covering approximately 55.6 kilometers (34.5 miles). This moderate trail offers an extensive ride through the region's diverse landscapes.
tips: The endless olive groves end up becoming hypnotic and relaxing. This special landscape is partly due to the distance between the olive trees, greater than in other types of crops.
From this viewpoint you can see the immense hills full of olive trees and Villanueva del Trabuco in the background, at the foot of the Sierra de Gorda and San Jorge.

The Fuente de los Cien Caños is an impressive natural monument located in Villanueva del Trabuco, on the limestone slope of the Sierra de San Jorge. The fountain is known for being the source of the Guadalhorce River, the longest and most voluminous river in the province of Malaga.

waypoints.name: Alto Viewpoint (Sierra San Jorge and Sierra Gorda)
